channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Mastering Azure management: A comparative analysis of leading cloud platforms
item.description: Azure’s growing adoption among companies leveraging cloud platforms highlights the increasing need for effective cloud resource management.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: WordPress.org statement threatens possible shutdown for all of 2025
item.description: Automattic CEO Matt Mullenweg on Friday announced a shutdown of almost all services on WordPress.org, the open source project site that’s the home of the software, plugins, and the WordPress community, but was unclear on when the shutdown would end.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia’s Run:ai acquisition waved through by EU
item.description: Nvidia will face no objections to its plan to acquire Israeli AI orchestration software vendor Run:ai Labs in Europe, after the European Commission gave the deal its approval Friday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Scholz dismisses Musk's assertion that only a far-right party can 'save' Germany
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Chancellor Olaf Scholz on Friday dismissed an assertion by Elon Musk that only a far-right party can “save Germany,” but said that freedom of opinion "also goes for multibillionaires.” Germany is expected to vote in an early election on Feb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Revolutionizing policing: Kazakhstan’s trailblazing Digital Policeman initiative
item.description: In the era of digital transformation, public safety stands at a critical crossroads. Law enforcement agencies globally are under increasing scrutiny to enhance transparency, efficiency, and trust within their communities.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Data-Driven Deliveries: Walmart's Catchment Area Model Revolutionizes Holiday Shopping
item.description: Walmart's innovative data science approach enables delivery to 12 million more households this holiday season, leveraging a new catchment area model that adapts to customer demand and needs.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: How OpenAI’s Jerry Tworek found a new way forward for large language models
item.description: The impressive intelligence gains in OpenAI’s models over time have mainly come from training them with progressively more training data, for longer amounts of time, and with massive computing power.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Australian who falsely claimed to have invented bitcoin is found in contempt of UK court
item.description: LONDON (AP) — An Australian computer scientist who falsely claimed to be the founder of the bitcoin cryptocurrency was found Thursday to be in contempt of an order of London's High Court and was sentenced to 12 months in prison, suspended for two years.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Elon Musk considers funding Nigel Farage's populist party, a move that could shake up UK politics
item.description: LONDON (AP) — It’s a photo that sent a tremor through British politics: Elon Musk flanked by British politician Nigel Farage and a wealthy backer, in front of a gilt-framed painting of a young Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apple complains Meta requests risk privacy in spat over EU efforts to widen access to iPhone tech
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple complained that requests from Meta Platforms for access to its operating software threaten user privacy, in a spat fueled by the European Union's intensifying efforts to get the iPhone maker to open up to products from tech rivals.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Getting started with Azure Managed Redis
item.description: Microsoft has made a lot of big bets in its preferred cloud-native infrastructure. You only need to look at .
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Go 1.24 brings full support for generic type aliases
item.description: Go 1.24, a planned update to Google‘s popular open-source programming language, has reached the release candidate stage.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Create searchable Bluesky bookmarks with R
item.description: The Bluesky social network is seeping into the mainstream, thanks in part to people unhappy with changes at X (formerly Twitter). “It’s not some untouched paradise, but it’s got a spark of something refreshing,” Jan Beger, global head of AI Advocacy at GE HealthCare, posted on LinkedIn last month.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Data on animal movements help Hungarian researchers create a swarm of autonomous drones
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Moving in a dense cloud, like throngs of people walking across a crowded public square, 100 drones maneuver through the night sky in Hungary's capital, the result of over a decade of research and experimentation that scientists believe could change the future of unmanned fli
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Snapchat is overhauling how influencers earn money on the platform
item.description: “I’m sorry WHO is using snapchat in the big year of 2024,” one X user asked. Turns out, a lot of people and for some, it’s proving lucrative. Influencer Julieanna Goddard, better known as YesJulz, recently posted her impressive Snapchat earnings to social media in response to the question.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: 4 key concepts for Rust beginners
item.description: Few languages are as highly esteemed by developers as Rust, which promises robust memory safety without compromising speed. Rust also has a steep learning curve, though, and some of its programming concepts can feel like a barrier to knowing it well.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: You have a license to code
item.description: One of the coolest things about software development is that you don’t need anyone’s permission to do it. If you need an application for your business or personal use, there isn’t a single thing stopping you from learning to code and writing it yourself.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The push to upskill the technology workforce in federal agencies
item.description: The federal government is facing a massive tech talent shortage, particularly as the development and use of AI continues to gain traction. The post The push to upskill the technology workforce in federal agencies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: DHS Science and Technology Directorate looks to the evolution of first responder communications
item.description: New realities of emergency work for emergency responders has necessitated increased use of data transmission of live incident video and file sharing. The post DHS Science and Technology Directorate looks to the evolution of first responder communications first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Schneider Electric shares energy-efficient data center designs
item.description: Schneider Electric has introduced AI-ready data center designs aimed at addressing the energy demands and sustainability challenges brought by artificial intelligence (AI). The new solutions are designed to enhance efficiency, sustainability and scalability for AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Steps to protect yourself while shopping on your phone this holiday season
item.description: The holiday season sees a rise in mobile shopping scams. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you learn how to stay safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU investigates TikTok over Romanian presidential election safeguards
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators said Tuesday they're investigating whether TikTok breached the bloc's digital rulebook by failing to deal with risks to Romania's presidential election, which has been thrown into turmoil over allegations of electoral violations and Russian meddling.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 reasons why 2025 will be the year of OpenTelemetry
item.description: Some open-source projects are spectacularly successful and become standard components of the IT infrastructure. Take Linux or Kubernetes, for example.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: US moves to tighten restrictions on China Telecom amid security fears
item.description: The US Commerce Department is intensifying its actions against China Telecom’s US unit over concerns that its cloud and internet services could be used to funnel American data to Beijing, according to a Reuters report.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Top 5 use cases for small language models
item.description: Since ChatGPT arrived in late 2022, large language models (LLMs) have continued to raise the bar for what generative AI systems can accomplish.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
